steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between the subject (Dr. Rubén G.) and the verb phrase that follows. The question tests whether punctuation belongs between a subject and its verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — semicolon between subject and verb: A semicolon separates two independent clauses. Placing one between a subject and its verb is never correct. ✗
  • Choice B — no punctuation: A subject and its verb in the same clause are not separated by any punctuation. No mark here keeps the sentence grammatically intact. ✓
  • Choice C — comma between subject and verb: A comma cannot separate a simple subject from its verb. ✗
  • Choice D — colon between subject and verb: A colon introduces a list or explanation after a complete clause, not between a subject and verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
